zh.lifeSummary: Miguel Antonio Alvarez Zapata於1020年出生。他和Sinforosa Amaya至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Miguel Antonio Alvarez Zapata于1020年出生。他和Sinforosa Amaya至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Alvarez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Ramon, Pedro, Miguel, Roberto.  Portuguese Catarina, Lidio, Ligia, Joaquim, Wenceslao.
Spanish (Álvarez): patronymic from the personal name Álvaro (see Alvaro ). Compare Alavarez , Albares , Albarez , Alvares , and Alverez .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Alverez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Alberto, Alfredo, Francisco, Mario, Miguel, Ramon, Carlos, Javier, Luis, Manuel, Marcelino.
Hispanic (Álverez): altered form of Spanish Álvarez (see Alvarez ).

variants.name: Albares
meanings: Spanish: variant of Álvarez (see Alvarez ).

variants.name: Alavarez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Álvarez (see Alvarez ).

variants.name: Alvaro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Raul, Adelaida, Alba, Alberto, Alejandro, Anaya, Benito, Celestino, Chavez, Emilia.  Italian Antonio, Alfonse, Angelo, Cesare, Giuseppe, Rocco, Sal.
Spanish and Portuguese (Álvaro); Italian: from the personal name Álvaro, Alvaro, which is of ancient Germanic (Visigothic) origin, but the exact etymology is not clear.

variants.name: Albarez
meanings: Spanish (Álbarez): variant of Álvarez (see Alvarez ). This surname is very rare in Spain.

nameMeanings.name: Zapata
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Miguel, Jaime, Jorge, Francisco, Julio, Mario.
Spanish and Galician: possibly also a habitational name from any of the places in Pontevedra and Ávila called Zapata.
Spanish: metonymic occupational name for a cobbler or shoemaker, from zapato ‘half boot’.

variants.name: Zapeta
meanings: Hispanic (Guatemala): altered form of Spanish   Zapata  .

variants.name: Zapanta
meanings: Hispanic (Philippines): possibly an altered form of Spanish   Zapata  .

nameMeanings.name: Miguel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical. See Michael .

variants.name: Michael
meanings: form of a common biblical name (meaning ‘who is like God?’ in Hebrew) borne by one of the archangels, the protector of the ancient Hebrews, who is also regarded as a saint of the Catholic Church. In the Middle Ages, Michael was regarded as captain of the heavenly host (see Revelation 12:7–9), symbol of the Church Militant, and patron of soldiers. He was often depicted bearing a flaming sword. The name is also borne by a Persian prince and ally of Belshazzar mentioned in the Book of Daniel. Since the early 1900s it has been one of the most enduringly popular boys' names in the English-speaking world.  See also  Michal .

nameMeanings.name: Antonio
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Spanish form of Anthony , from Latin Antonius, also used in English‐speaking countries.

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: The usual English form of the old Roman family name Antonius, which is of uncertain (probably Etruscan) origin. The spelling with -th- (not normally reflected in the pronunciation) represents a learned but erroneous attempt to associate it with Greek anthos ‘flower’. In the post-classical period it was a common name, borne by various early saints, most notably a 3rd-century Egyptian hermit monk, who is regarded as the founder of Christian monasticism.
